news.title: Blockbuster US job gains ruffle Wall Street
news.summary: Forecast-beating US jobs data rattled Wall Street on Friday as investors saw increased chances of an interest rate hike.Data showed the US economy added 172,000 jobs in May, far more than the 80,000 expected by economists polled by Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

news.title: Don't expect to see SpaceX on the S&P 500 so soon after IPO
news.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices stated that it will not be changing its rules to fast-track SpaceX (SPAX.PVT) and other mega-IPOs into the S&P 500 index (^GSPC). Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Vanity Fair contributing editor Bethany McLean discuss SpaceX's valuation in the lead up to the space company's public launch a week from now. JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon hosted Elon Musk as part of SpaceX's (SPAX.PVT) pre-IPO roadshow, as the company sets its sights on an IPO price of $135 per share next Friday, June 12.

news.title: Nvidia's Huang arrives in South Korea with 'surprises', bets on robotics
news.summary: Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang arrived in Seoul Friday for a packed schedule of meetings with tech leaders, promising "some surprises" for South Korea while predicting robotics will be the country's next major growth sector.Huang said robotics would be the "next major sector here in South Korea", and that the country is "extraordinary at manufacturing, mechatronics, and also artificial intelligence".

news.title: Strong Jobs Report Puts Fed Rate Hike In Play; S&P 500 Futures Slip (Live Coverage)
news.summary: The May jobs report showed stronger-than-expected hiring as the unemployment rate held steady.  The data strengthens the hand of Federal Reserve hawks, setting the stage for a shift to a tightening bias at the June 17 policy meeting, the first under new Chairman Kevin Warsh.  The S&P 500 slipped following the jobs report, putting the nine-week winning streak for the benchmark index in jeopardy.

news.title: How Low Can The XRP Price Go?
news.summary: XRP's price peaked at $3.65 in July 2025 and is now trading near $1.12, a decline of roughly 70% from that high. The six-month window referenced here, which captures a 47% drop, places the current episode firmly within a pattern XRP has repeated three times in the past five years. The question is not whether this kind of drop is unusual for XRP. It is not. The question is how much further it can extend.
